_comment: Corpus file registry (TEMPLATE). Real-world SOLIDWORKS files are referenced by ABSOLUTE PATH rather than copied in-tree, so the repo stays small and IP-clean (see docs/LEGAL.md). The test harness reads this config and runs the real-file Layer-2/3 tests against each existing path; entries whose path does not exist are skipped. To test against your OWN legitimately-obtained files, add entries with a short tag and a local absolute path (those local edits are yours to keep; do NOT commit private paths upstream).
_format_note: Each entry: { tag, path, doc_type (part|assembly|drawing), expected_format (modern|ole2|opc), sw_version_estimate, license_status (synthetic|owner), notes }. expected_format/sw_version_estimate are best guesses; the harness verifies and corrects them.

files.notes: EXAMPLE entry. Replace with a local absolute path to one of YOUR OWN SOLIDWORKS files to enable real-file tests. Skipped automatically when the path does not exist.

SYNTHETIC (no third-party data). Generated by a corpus-generator script: an empty weldment (1 extruded body + weldment feature) with a user-text cut-list property. A clean small moCutListPropContainer_c example for cut-list structural-walker + value-editor tests. Path is repo-relative; the harness also accepts absolute. Not distributed in the repo (generate locally).

_to_add_later: A SLDPRT from an older SW release (e.g. 2020) to test the version range
A SLDDRW with multiple sheets to test drawing-side round-trip
A SLDASM with external references (to validate the assembly tree + ref tables)
Two saves of the SAME part 10 seconds apart, for the save-nondeterminism baseline
